author: Viggo Meesters
description: Open shell, batch, PowerShell, and AutoHotkey files as read-only source with outline, search, and safety hints. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Viggo Meesters
manifest.description: Open scripts and safe dotfiles as read-only source with outline, search, and safety hints.
manifest.name: Script Viewer

name: Script Viewer

releases.author: viggomeesters
releases.description: Add a file-open fallback so supported dotfiles such as .gitignore and .env.example are rerouted from the default text editor into Script Viewer even when the File Explorer bypasses extension routing.

releases.author: viggomeesters
releases.description: Route safe dotfiles such as .env, .env.example, .gitignore, .dockerignore, .npmrc, .nvmrc, and .yarnrc into Script Viewer instead of falling through to external viewers. Adds dotenv and ignore-file parsing modes while preserving read-only, never-execute behavior.

releases.author: viggomeesters
releases.description:
Initial release of Script Viewer.

- Read-only viewer for .sh, .bash, .zsh, .bat, .cmd, .ps1, .ahk, .command, and .bats files.
- Adds source view, outline, search/filter, interpreter summary, refresh, render caps, and safety hints.
- Never executes scripts, starts terminals/subprocesses, writes files, uses clipboard APIs, or makes runtime network requests.

Checks:
- npm run build
- npx tsc --noEmit
- npm test
